articles.title: Khordha orders morning classes from 6:30 AM - 9:30 AM amid severe heatwave - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
In light of the severe heatwave affecting citizens in Odisha, the District Collector of Khordha on Thursday issued new timings for all schools and Anganwadi centres in Khordha district. This decision comes as temperatures in the region soar to alarming levels, with the city of Bhubaneswar and surrounding areas experiencing unprecedented heat.
Starting from tomorrow April 24th, 2026, all schools (Government, Private, and Aided) and Anganwadi Centres in
Khordha will operate from 6:30 AM to 9:30 AM to protect students and staff from the extreme heat.
The heatwave has pushed the temperatures to over 42°C, making it one of the hottest days of the year. Bhubaneswar has recorded 41.8°C, and cities like Jharsuguda and Hirakud have experienced temperatures surpassing 43°C, triggering a state-wide alert.
Meanwhile, the IMD (India Meteorological Department) has also issued a heatwave warning for five districts, including Khordha, Balangir, Jharsuguda, Sambalpur and Kendujhar. As the temperature continues to rise, the public is urged to take precautions.
Earlier, the Cuttack district administration on April 19 revised the functioning hours of schools and Anganwadi centres to ensure the safety and well-being of children.
As per the order, all government, government-aided, and private schools in Cuttack will conduct classes from 6:30 AM and close by 9:00 AM until further notice.
The Office of the District Magistrate and Collector, Cuttack, issued an official order on April 19, directing all educational institutions in the district to operate only during early morning hours.
This shift in school timings aims to reduce the exposure of children to the scorching sun and ensure their safety during the ongoing heatwave.
